summary:
The World Health Organization does not recommend proactive screening of family members as a core mental health intervention. Instead, it focuses on individual risk assessment, such as asking about family history, promoting social support, ensuring human rights, and enhancing communication within healthcare settings—none of which involve systematically identifying or testing relatives' mental health statuses. 

The summary must be exactly two sentences long and contain only essential facts related directly to the clinical context presented above.

Only one option among those listed fails to align with actual WHO guidance—that being 'screening family members'. This activity has no basis in current WHO recommendations due to lack of explicit endorsement or documentation across official publications focused on global mental health strategies. Screening family members is therefore excluded from officially recommended interventions based on available literature.
